title: How journalists cover the Middle East war despite restrictions and the rise of AI-generated images
subhed: In this crisis that spans multiple regions, reporters are facing a tough challenge. Iran is almost entirely closed to foreign journalists, while in the Gulf countries, Israel and Lebanon, varying degrees of censorship and restrictions have been imposed on the press. At the same time, fake news about the conflict has spread across the internet.

France eyes social media ban for under-15s
subhed: If the bill is passed, all social media platforms will refuse new users under 15, and pre-existing accounts that belong to under-15s will be suspended. Opponents argue that lawmakers must hold the platforms responsible, and not simply ban children from social media.

title: 'In their conflict with Iran, the US and Israel are pursuing divergent war aims'
subhed: While Benjamin Netanyahu seeks to topple a regime that the vast majority of Israelis view as an existential threat, Donald Trump is driven by absolute mercantilism, geopolitical expert Frédéric Encel argues for Le Monde.

title: French archives of Spaniards who fled Franco's dictatorship now available online
subhed: Starting on March 30, more than 180,000 records became accessible on the website of the French Office for the Protection of Refugees and Stateless Persons (OFPRA), thanks to funding from Spain aimed at preserving the memory of 40 years of exile in France.

title: The French cadets suspected of setting a boat ablaze in Japan
subhed: An investigation is underway into how four cadets from France's elite Saint-Cyr military academy came to be accused of setting a boat on fire while on leave in Okinawa in 2024. The incident has fueled local distrust of military personnel on the island.

Ireland gives Trump a lesson on Europe
subhed: During the traditional Saint Patrick's Day visit to the White House by the Irish prime minister, Micheál Martin defended his British counterpart and praised the virtues of Europe in the face of criticism from the US president.

title: Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
subhed: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

title: Escaping Putin's Russia: The father and daughter persecuted over a drawing
subhed: Alexei Moskalyov and his daughter, Maria, are symbolic victims of the Kremlin's repression. The father was jailed after posting an anti-war drawing his teenage daughter made at school. They have just arrived in France and are under humanitarian protection.
